tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: Generate a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: GoogleSearch
tools.description: Search the web for relevant information.

inputs.name: k
inputs.description: The number of top search results to return.

dialogs.content: Identify the tissue, determine the stain in it.

dialogs.thought: Start with a global overview to understand what tissue/organ is shown and how the figure is organized across panels.

content.content: A two-panel histology figure showing embryo heart tissue sections. The panels appear to compare two genotypes (left vs right). The tissue shows thick muscular walls and trabeculated regions consistent with myocardium/ventricular wall architecture.

dialogs.thought: Use OCR to confirm the genotype labels and any text that may help interpret the panels.
Assess staining appearance across the tissue sections to determine the stain type based on characteristic color patterns.

arguments.attribute: dominant stain colors and appearance (e.g., pink cytoplasm, purple/blue nuclei) indicative of histology stain

content.content: The sections show predominantly pink/red cytoplasm and extracellular matrix with purple/blue nuclei, consistent with a hematoxylin-and-eosin (H&E) color pattern rather than DAB immunostaining or trichrome.

dialogs.thought: Optionally sanity-check the color-to-stain mapping with a brief web lookup.

arguments.query: H&E stain appearance blue purple nuclei pink cytoplasm eosin hematoxylin

content.content: H&E staining: hematoxylin stains nuclei blue-purple and eosin stains cytoplasm/extracellular matrix pink; this is the classic routine histology color pattern.

dialogs.thought: Compare ventricular morphology between the left (crt+/-) and right (crt-/-) panels by visually inspecting wall thickness, compact layer development, and fenestration/trabeculation.

arguments.attribute: compare ventricular wall thickness, compact layer growth, and fenestration/trabeculation between crt+/- (left) and crt-/- (right)

content.content: Compared with the crt+/- panel, the crt-/- panel shows a thinner ventricular wall with reduced/attenuated compact layer development and more prominent trabeculation with increased fenestration/porous spaces in the myocardium.

dialogs.content: The figure shows heart (myocardial/ventricular) tissue stained with H&E. OCR confirms the left panel is crt+/- and the right panel is crt-/-. Compared with crt+/-, the crt-/- heart shows thinner ventricular walls, impaired compact layer growth, and increased fenestration/trabeculation, consistent with abnormal ventricular development.
